title: Add Google, Apple, Microsoft, and Passkey login options on Register Step 2 and Login page

requirements.summary: Replace single 'Verify with Internet Identity' button on Register Step 2 (Authenticate screen) with four distinct authentication option buttons: Google, Apple, Microsoft, and Continue with Passkey, each triggering the Internet Identity login flow
acceptanceCriteria: Step 2 (Authenticate) screen shows four separate buttons: Google, Apple, Microsoft, and Continue with Passkey
Each button has its respective brand logo/icon (Google multicolor G, Apple black logo, Microsoft colored squares, passkey person icon)
The single 'Verify with Internet Identity' button is no longer displayed
Clicking any of the four buttons triggers the Internet Identity authentication flow
After successful authentication, registration proceeds to Step 3 (Success screen) as before
Error state ('Authentication cancelled or failed') still shows if login is cancelled, with option to retry
Layout matches the id.ai 'Choose method' style: Google, Apple, Microsoft in a row, then 'Continue with Passkey' as a full-width button below

file_operations.description: Create a reusable AuthMethodButtons component that renders four authentication option buttons (Google, Apple, Microsoft, and Continue with Passkey) in the id.ai 'Choose method' layout style. Each button displays the appropriate brand logo/icon and triggers a callback when clicked. The component accepts an onMethodClick callback prop that receives the method name and executes the Internet Identity login flow. Use Tailwind classes for styling: display Google, Apple, and Microsoft buttons in a horizontal row (flex gap), then render 'Continue with Passkey' as a full-width button below. Ensure buttons are visually distinct with proper spacing, hover states, and accessible labels.
Add Google multicolor 'G' logo SVG icon for the Google authentication button. Use the standard Google brand colors (blue, red, yellow, green) in the official Google logo design.
Add Apple black logo SVG icon for the Apple authentication button. Use the standard Apple logo silhouette in black (#000000).
Add Microsoft colored squares logo SVG icon for the Microsoft authentication button. Use the standard Microsoft logo with four colored squares (red, green, blue, yellow) arranged in a 2x2 grid.
Add passkey/person icon SVG for the 'Continue with Passkey' authentication button. Use a simple person silhouette or key icon that represents passkey authentication.

In the RegisterPage component, replace the single 'Verify with Internet Identity' button on Step 2 (Authenticate screen) with the new AuthMethodButtons component. Remove the existing blue button that displays 'Verify with Internet Identity'. Import and render <AuthMethodButtons onMethodClick={handleMethodClick} /> where handleMethodClick calls the existing handleAuth function to trigger Internet Identity login. Ensure the error state ('Authentication cancelled or failed') and retry button remain unchanged. Preserve all existing registration flow logic (Step 1 form validation, Step 3 success screen, backend actor calls). Do not modify Steps 1 or 3.

requirements.summary: Replace single Internet Identity authentication button on Login page with four distinct authentication option buttons: Google, Apple, Microsoft, and Continue with Passkey, each triggering the Internet Identity login flow
acceptanceCriteria: Login page shows four separate buttons: Google, Apple, Microsoft, and Continue with Passkey
Each button has its respective brand logo/icon
The single Internet Identity button is replaced by these four options
Clicking any of the four buttons triggers the Internet Identity authentication flow
After successful authentication, user is logged in and redirected to dashboard as before
Layout matches the id.ai 'Choose method' style: Google, Apple, Microsoft in a row, then 'Continue with Passkey' as full-width button below

file_operations.description: In the LoginPage component, replace the existing single Internet Identity authentication button with the new AuthMethodButtons component. Remove the current login button and import <AuthMethodButtons onMethodClick={handleMethodClick} /> where handleMethodClick triggers the existing Internet Identity login flow (calling login() from useInternetIdentity hook). Preserve all existing login logic: fetch caller's profile from backend actor after successful authentication, redirect to player dashboard on success, show error message if no account is found. Ensure the 'Don't have an account? Register here' link remains visible below the authentication buttons. Do not modify the overall page layout or navigation header.
